description: Rebuild thread/revThread/succNum/lastSucc after the parent-chain reversal, and expose the public updateTree wrapper matching the pivot-phase contract.
commit: feat: add thread/succNum rebuild and updateTree wrapper
steps: Implement `func (s *state) rebuildThread(entering, leaving, join, leavingSide int)`. Splice the moved subtree out of its old thread position and back in at its new position (after its new parent's current lastSucc, or the new parent itself). Update revThread to stay the inverse. Recompute lastSucc for ancestors on both old and new sides: walk up from the old position decrementing lastSucc until reaching an ancestor whose lastSucc did not previously end inside the moved subtree; walk up from the new position extending lastSucc as needed. Update succNum: walk from old parent up to root subtracting succNumOf(movedSubtree); walk from new parent up to root adding the same amount.

Implement the public wrapper `func (s *state) updateTree(entering, leaving, join, leavingSide int)` matching the pivot-phase contract. Body: if entering == leaving call s.flipArcState(entering) and return; else call s.reverseParentChain(entering, leaving, join, leavingSide) then s.rebuildThread(entering, leaving, join, leavingSide).

Create solver_update_tree_test.go with `TestUpdateTree_Degenerate`: arcState[entering] = stateLower; call updateTree(entering, entering, join, 0). Assert arcState[entering]==stateUpper and parent/pred/thread all unchanged.

Add `TestUpdateTree_Pivot`: hand-crafted 5-node spanning tree, valid pivot with entering != leaving. Call updateTree. Assert: (a) thread walked from root visits each node in [0, totalNodes) exactly once (valid preorder permutation); (b) revThread is the inverse of thread; (c) succNum[root] == totalNodes; (d) arcState[entering]==stateTree, arcState[leaving] is stateLower or stateUpper.

Add `TestUpdateTree_LastSuccInvariant`: after the pivot test above, for every node u assert that s.lastSucc[u] is the last node in preorder that is a descendant of u, i.e. following thread starting at u for succNum[u]-1 steps lands on lastSucc[u].
acceptance_criteria: solver_update_tree.go defines `func (s *state) rebuildThread(entering, leaving, join, leavingSide int)` and `func (s *state) updateTree(entering, leaving, join, leavingSide int)`.
updateTree handles the degenerate pivot (entering == leaving) via flipArcState alone.
